assertionResults.title: Scenario switching updates assumptions and preserves user inputs
assertionResults.reason: With a sample position of 500000 BETTER and a $10000 vault deposit, the same input values remained in place while Conservative, Base, and Upside updated all five assumption dimensions plus the dependent projection cards/table together.

steps.action: Open #tokenomics and enter 500000 BETTER plus a $10000 vault deposit in Scenario Projections
steps.expected: The UI should show the user's tier/allocation preview and keep those values available during scenario changes.
steps.observed: The page resolved the sample balance to Whale, showed 1.6× tier weight and $16000 effective allocation, and kept both inputs visible in the form.

steps.expected: Assumptions and dependent outputs update together while the entered inputs persist.
steps.observed: Prediction Markets changed from 15B to 5B, Annualized Protocol Revenue changed from $8.5M to $1.2M, and the 500000 / $10000 inputs stayed unchanged.

steps.expected: The other scenario assumptions should appear for Hyperliquid/HyperEVM, social vaults, AI-agent tooling, and enterprise rails without clearing the user's inputs.
steps.observed: Upside showed 50B prediction-market volume, 12% Hyperliquid share, $100M vault AUM, 5000 agents, and $15M enterprise revenue; returning to Base restored the base assumptions while preserving the same inputs.

evidence.network: No requests were captured after clearing logs; the scenario updates were client-side.

assertionResults.title: Scenario outputs remain logically coherent
assertionResults.reason: The comparison table stayed monotonic for every displayed output, so there were no unexplained inverse metrics across conservative, base, and upside.

steps.action: Review the Cross-Scenario Comparison table using the same sample inputs
steps.expected: Outputs should remain logically ordered or explicitly explain inverse metrics.
steps.observed: All visible metrics increased from Conservative to Base to Upside with no inverse case shown.

steps.expected: Major projection rows should not contradict the scenario ordering.
steps.observed: Annualized Protocol Revenue $1.2M < $8.5M < $45M; Monthly Active Terminal Users 2000 < 15000 < 80000; Total Vault AUM $5M < $25M < $100M; Active Autonomous Agents 50 < 500 < 5000; Enterprise Revenue $500K < $3M < $15M.

evidence.calculationNotes: All five displayed outputs were strictly ordered Conservative < Base < Upside; no inverse metric required an explanatory note.

assertionResults.title: Fee-stack examples and value-flow mapping are both explicit
assertionResults.reason: The tokenomics flow separated each fee line with worked examples and then mapped Consumer, Pro / Prosumer, Whale, and Enterprise flows to explicit destinations with maturity badges.

steps.expected: Trading tax, Lite Mode fees, vault performance fees, whale premium fee advantage, and future revenue lines should be individually explained with examples.
steps.observed: The section listed Trading Tax (Buy/Sell), Lite Mode Per-Fill Fee, Vault Performance Fee, Whale Premium Fee Advantage, Agent Transaction Fees, and Enterprise Data & API Licensing, each with a worked example and Live/In Progress/Planned badge.

steps.expected: Consumer, pro/prosumer, whale, and enterprise revenue flows should map to treasury/sinks/reinvestment with explicit maturity labels.
steps.observed: Consumer mapped to Protocol treasury, Pro / Prosumer to Treasury + vault manager rewards + token sinks, Whale to Treasury + reinvestment pool + burn mechanics, and Enterprise to Treasury + infrastructure reinvestment, each with a visible status label.

assertionResults.title: The roadmap token utility surface covers the required agent-native mechanics
assertionResults.reason: The utility surface covered every required mechanic and attached maturity labels plus caveats/dependencies to the future-facing items.

steps.expected: The surface should cover strategy agents, bonded quality controls, delegation/backing, research/data bounties, premium API or agent lanes, LLM/inference credits, and whale-exclusive products where applicable.
steps.observed: The section showed Strategy Agents, Bonded Agent Registry, Agent Delegation & Backing, Research & Data Bounties, Premium API & Agent Lanes, LLM & Inference Credits, and Whale Exclusive Products.

steps.expected: Future-facing utility should expose maturity status and dependency/caveat framing.
steps.observed: Cards visibly used Planned or Speculative badges and included caveats such as infrastructure readiness, slashing-rule design, and dependence on Full-Stack AI Intelligence or the whale-first tier ladder.

assertionResults.title: Key token and projection numbers expose source or assumption cues
assertionResults.reason: Supply math, tier thresholds, and projection cards all showed visible evidence hooks that identify whether a number is canonical, doc-backed, or scenario-based.

steps.action: Inspect token supply/allocation and threshold surfaces
steps.expected: Key supply math and threshold numbers should show a source or assumption cue.
steps.observed: The allocation table paired rows like Community & Ecosystem 40% / 400000000 with BETTER Tokenomics source hooks, and tier cards such as Lite ≥ 50000 BETTER showed BETTER Docs as of 2026-Q1.

steps.action: Inspect projection cards inside Scenario Projections
steps.expected: Major projection numbers should disclose that they are scenario-based or illustrative.
steps.observed: Projection cards such as Annualized Protocol Revenue $8.5M and Enterprise Revenue $3M were labeled with BETTER Scenario Model hooks, while the user allocation preview used a Your Inputs illustrative cue.

frictions.description: agent-browser recording runs in a fresh capture context, so after saving the scenario-switch recording I re-entered the sample inputs in the main session before taking the final verification screenshots.
frictions.resolution: Repeated the fill-and-switch flow outside the recording context and confirmed the same 500000 BETTER / $10000 values persisted across Conservative, Base, and Upside.

summary: Tested 5 assigned browser assertions for tokenomics scenarios/value flow: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Scenario switching preserved entered inputs while updating the five assumption dimensions, the comparison table stayed monotonic, and the fee/value-flow, utility, and source-cue surfaces were all explicit and maturity-labeled.
